End Game
The living room, mostly used for guests, is filled with chairs.
Outside the window, you can see the adjacent high-rise buildings below. It's normal for these kinds of buildings to get more expensive as you go higher, and this room isn't cheap either.
It's inside Trump Tower.
This is the director's room. It feels a bit too luxurious for a secondary house where he just stays occasionally.
It seemed like he had regained his energy after all the sources of stress were cleared away, but today he looks even more exhausted.
You seem to lack energy.
He looks older than usual. The only thing troubling the director right now is his daughter.
Sure, she's at an age where it's understandable, but I don't feel like she's aging. The rumors about actresses keep coming out, which isn’t surprising.
You seem quite tired, don’t you?
Yeah.
Actually, I haven’t heard from Patricia since then.
Are you still in a parent-child quarrel?
Yeah.
I don't mean to, but it's been a week since the phone stopped connecting.
I'm worried she might have gotten involved in some incident.
If you're that worried, why not contact the police? I don't expect them to search vigorously, though.
Yeah.
But if it's just a whim on her part, it would affect a lot of people.
Still, it’s too late once something happens. If I had a few more connections, I could help you.
No, it's fine. Thank you.
I'll wait a bit longer.
She's a troublesome child.
She probably isn't even thinking about how much her parents are worried.
It's true we had a fight, but it’s impossible for her not to contact me for a week.
Hasn't she already gone back to Boston?
No, that's not the case. I checked.
Well then, why not ask her place of stay?
The thing is, she’s at my vacation home alone.
I can't reach the caretaker of the villa, so I don't know what she's doing right now.
That's worrying.
I just hope she isn't in any danger.
I hope nothing has happened.
Guy had an accident somewhere around Colorado Springs, didn't he?
Yeah, somewhere in the mountains around there.
Actually, my daughter's destination is Colorado Springs. I have this uneasy feeling.
It’s unlikely that my daughter is involved in Guy’s accidental death, but I just can’t shake this anxiety.
I can understand how you feel.
Death can be so sudden, can't it? Even with a perfectly healthy body, Guy couldn’t escape death.
In this world, just living safely is a miracle.
I haven't seen Jennifer lately. I just can't bring myself to face her.
No, please don’t hold back with me.
Yeah, I get it, but...
It's hard for me to hear about you from Jennifer.
I must have liked Jennifer, I guess. So I don't want to see her talk about you with shining eyes.
What am I even saying?
You must be tired.
I'll step back; you should get along with Jennifer.
Meryl might already be back. I think I got a call about that.
What happened with Meryl and the director?
That was also the trigger for the fight with Patricia.
I think it probably ended a long time ago.
You know, there was a time I thought about marrying Meryl. But, it didn't happen because Patricia strongly opposed it.
Still, we've been lovers until now.
That was just a rotten bond, huh. Meryl said she wanted to find the daughter she had given up for adoption long ago, and since then it's been like this.
From now on, we'll just be work partners teaming up.
I heard at the funeral that Guy and Kristy are the ones behind the serial murder case.
But there’s no evidence for that.
True. But now I can't shake off the uneasy feeling.
I mean, I can't help but think... what if Patricia also fell victim to the criminal's fangs?
You'll be just fine.
Really, do you really think so?
Getting older makes you paranoid, it's a hassle, there’s no peace of mind.
It seems like you’re quite worked up about not being able to contact Patricia. Don't you think of going to look for her yourself?
If it were really possible, I would want to fly out to find Patricia. I wonder why she’s in New York.
Wasn't the director and Jennifer's relationship something else? Or did I just think too much?
Naturally, one would think it was a suicide or an accident. But I can't seem to accept that.
The police and related parties seem to have concluded that Guy is the serial murderer.
But I can't accept such a rash conclusion.
I have to prove that Guy is definitely not the culprit.
It seems the director really liked Jennifer. But what about her?
Jennifer didn't try to accept the director's feelings. Is it my fault?
Does Jennifer think about me?
I wonder what she thinks of me.
Am I recognized as a more important man than the director?
I wonder if he’s happy about Gai’s death.
Meryl completely believed that Gai was the one who killed Cindy.
The relationship with the director had really been cold for a long time.
Is that why I thought about looking for my daughter?
Meryl lost both her lover and her daughter. What will she live for now?
The police seem to have decided that Gai is the culprit. It's true he had a strong side.
If this ends the serial killings, it will really mean Gai is the killer.
No way, did Patricia also fall into the culprit's hands?
Am I overthinking this?
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
